summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a legendary May ’77 show featuring a smoking Sugaree, towering Peggy‑O and an unstoppable Terrapin > Playin’/UJB stretch.
review: The SHN‑sourced soundboard is praised as crisp and “mind‑blowing,” delivering every nuance of Garcia’s lead work and the band’s tight improvisations. Musically the concert shines with multiple fan‑favorite moments – a three‑part Sugaree, soaring Peggy‑O, a flawless Terrapin Station → Playing in the Band into Uncle John’s, plus standout China Doll, The Wheel and Dancin’. Overall reviewers are uniformly ecstatic, calling it one of the best May ’77 performances ever recorded.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 5 stars, lauding both performance and audio; recording is consistently described as excellent soundboard quality, giving an exceptional combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Repeated references to “crisp,” “mind‑blowing” SHN soundboard and “excellent sound quality” indicate a clean, well‑balanced mix with no noted dropouts or distortion.

standout_songs: Terrapin Station > Playing in the Band
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as “no slacker” with classic ’77 staples, while the second set is an uninterrupted adventure—Terrapin > Playin’ into Uncle John’s and a gorgeous China Doll that keeps the momentum soaring.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for sweet, smoky guitar tone on Sugaree, wizardry on Peggy‑O, slide work, and an overall “on fire” performance throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned for closing with a rocking Dancin’ and contributing strong rhythm/vocal support.
